user_query:
I need to push back (professionally) on an internal proposal to sponsor electric racing. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and produce the specified outputs under output/:

1) Discovery via command output: Run a case-insensitive grep over all .txt and .md files in input/ for the terms: Formula E, electric, EV, battery, cost, sustainability. Save the exact raw command output to output/grep_output.txt. Example pattern: grep -niE '(Formula E|electric|EV|battery|cost|sustainability)' on the input/ directory.

2) Structured extraction: Parse the saved grep output and build output/extracted_lines.csv with columns: source_file,line_number,match_term,text. Determine match_term as the first of the six terms that appears in each line according to the regex order (Formula E, electric, EV, battery, cost, sustainability). For each row, open the referenced source file and verify the full line text matches exactly (no truncation). Include at least 8 rows spanning at least two different source files. Keep the text field as the exact line content from the file.

3) Email drafting: Using the extracted lines, draft a concise email to the Racing Partnerships Team that argues against sponsoring electric racing right now and reinforces our ICE-focused strategy. Requirements for the email (save to output/email_draft.txt):
- 150–200 words, professional and constructive tone.
- Include at least three short, verbatim quotes from the source materials, each cited inline as [filename:line].
- Summarize concrete concerns grounded in the extracted evidence (e.g., cost, battery logistics, sustainability nuances) and close with a clear next step (e.g., request for a cost-benefit review of ICE programs).

Deliverables:
- output/grep_output.txt (raw command output)
- output/extracted_lines.csv (verified, structured lines)
- output/email_draft.txt (final email)

Use the local files to ensure accuracy; do not rely on web sources. If grep returns unexpected output or nothing, analyze and adjust your pattern so that the final CSV still meets the minimum rows requirement. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
